title: Health Insurance and Health Outcomes: Evidence from a Randomized Medicaid Expansion

sections.label: sec:background

sections.title: Appendix B: Extended Balance and Robustness
sections.label: sec:appendix-b-health

tables.caption: First Stage: Effect of Lottery Selection on Insurance Coverage
tables.label: tab:first-stage

qa.question: What is the compliance rate (first-stage coefficient)?
What is the first-stage F-statistic in the baseline specification?
Is the first stage robust to adding controls?
qa.answer: Yes, coefficient is stable at 0.251--0.258 across all specifications

tables.caption: Summary Statistics by Treatment and Control Groups
tables.label: tab:summary-stats

qa.question: How many individuals are in the treatment group?
What is the mean age in the control group?
Are baseline health measures balanced across groups?
What is the mean number of chronic conditions in the treatment group?
What is the response rate for the 12-month survey?
Is there differential attrition by treatment status?
qa.answer: No, the difference in response rates is 0.007 (SE 0.006)

qa.question: What is the ITT effect on outpatient visits?
What is the ITT effect on ER visits?
Is the ITT effect on hospital admissions statistically significant?
qa.answer: No, the estimate is 0.014 with SE 0.011

qa.question: What is the control mean of outpatient visits?

tables.label: tab:utilization-iv

qa.question: What is the IV effect of Medicaid on outpatient visits?
What is the IV effect of Medicaid on ER visits?
What is the control mean of ER visits?

tables.caption: Effect of Medicaid on Health Outcomes
tables.label: tab:health-outcomes

qa.question: Does Medicaid improve self-reported health?
qa.answer: Yes, ITT effect is 0.084 (p<0.01) on 1-5 scale

qa.question: Does Medicaid reduce depression?
qa.answer: Yes, PHQ-8 score decreases by 0.482 points (ITT) or 1.868 points (IV)

qa.question: Does Medicaid affect blood pressure?
What is the control mean of the PHQ-8 depression score?

tables.caption: Effect of Medicaid on Financial Strain and Medical Debt
tables.label: tab:financial-outcomes

qa.question: Does Medicaid reduce medical debt?
What share of the control group has any medical debt?
What is the IV effect on difficulty paying medical bills?

tables.caption: Heterogeneity in Treatment Effects by Subgroup
tables.label: tab:heterogeneity

qa.question: Are treatment effects larger for older individuals?
qa.answer: Yes, the ITT on self-rated health increases from 0.054 (age 19-34) to 0.114 (age 50-64)

qa.question: Are treatment effects larger for sicker individuals?
qa.answer: Yes, the ITT on depression score is -0.841 for 3+ chronic conditions vs. -0.218 for none

qa.question: Are results robust to using probit instead of OLS?
qa.answer: Yes, probit marginal effects are very similar (0.029 vs 0.031)

qa.question: What are the Lee bounds for the depression screen effect?
qa.answer: Lower bound: -0.048, Upper bound: -0.021, both significant

qa.question: What is the annual government spending on Medicaid per enrollee?
What is the net benefit per enrollee?
What is the value of improved mental health per enrollee?
What is the joint F-test p-value for baseline balance?
What fraction of the treatment group smokes?

equations.label: eq:itt
equations.description: Intent-to-treat specification: $Y_i$ is the outcome, $\text{Lottery}_i$ is the random lottery indicator, $X_i$ are baseline covariates.

qa.question: What is the intent-to-treat estimating equation?
qa.answer: Y_i = alpha + beta * Lottery_i + X_i'gamma + epsilon_i

equations.label: eq:iv
equations.description: Two-stage least squares: Medicaid enrollment instrumented by lottery selection. $\delta$ is the LATE for compliers.

qa.question: What is the instrument for Medicaid enrollment?
What does the IV estimate identify?
qa.answer: The LATE (local average treatment effect) for compliers

equations.label: eq:late
equations.description: LATE equals the ITT effect divided by the first-stage compliance rate. Under monotonicity, this identifies the causal effect for compliers.

equations.label: eq:compliance
equations.description: First-stage compliance rate: the fraction of lottery winners who enroll in Medicaid beyond what the control group enrolls.

qa.question: What is the compliance rate?

equations.label: eq:monotonicity
equations.description: Monotonicity assumption: no defiers — lottery selection weakly increases Medicaid enrollment for everyone.

equations.label: eq:power
equations.description: Sample size formula for detectable effect $\delta_{MDE}$ given significance $\alpha$, power $\kappa$, and treatment share $p$.

equations.label: eq:het
equations.description: Heterogeneity specification: interaction of lottery with subgroup indicator $S_i$ (e.g., age category, chronic conditions).

equations.label: eq:fwer
equations.description: Benjamini-Hochberg step-up procedure for controlling the false discovery rate across $K$ outcome tests.

equations.label: eq:lee-bounds
equations.latex: \text{LB} = E[Y_i | Z_i = 1, Y_i \leq F_{Y|Z=1}^{-1}(1-p)], \quad \text{UB} = E[Y_i | Z_i = 1, Y_i \geq F_{Y|Z=1}^{-1}(p)]
equations.description: Lee (2009) bounds for attrition: trimming the outcome distribution of the treatment group to match control response rates.

equations.label: eq:qaly
equations.description: Welfare formula: value of QALY gains ($v \cdot \Delta Q$) plus uncompensated care offset plus insurance value minus government cost.

qa.question: What are the components of the welfare formula?
qa.answer: QALY gains, uncompensated care offset, insurance value of risk reduction, minus government fiscal cost

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: Randomized lottery for Medicaid enrollment, using lottery selection as instrument for Medicaid coverage

qa.question: What is the compliance rate?
qa.answer: 25.8 percent (lottery winners were 0.258 more likely to enroll)

qa.question: Does Medicaid improve self-reported health?
qa.answer: Yes, by 0.326 points on a 1-5 scale (IV estimate)

qa.question: Does Medicaid affect clinical biomarkers?
qa.answer: No statistically significant effects on blood pressure, cholesterol, or HbA1c

qa.question: Does Medicaid reduce financial strain?
qa.answer: Yes, medical debt decreases by $1,211 per enrollee (IV estimate)

qa.question: What is the compliance rate (first-stage coefficient)?
What is the first-stage F-statistic in the baseline specification?
Is the first stage robust to adding controls?
qa.answer: Yes, coefficient is stable at 0.251--0.258 across all specifications
